revision.comment: Text replacement - "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA ==" to "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]"
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Awa-jinja]][[en:Awa Shrine]][[fr:Awa-jinja]][[ja:安房神社]][[zh:安房神社]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Japan|qq=}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Awa Jinja
安房神社 | image = Precincts of Awa-jinja.JPG | caption = Precincts of Awa Jinja {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240|qq=}} | map_type = Japan Chiba Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|55|20.80|N|139|50|12.25|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline|qq=}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Futodama|Ame-no-Futodama]] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 589 Daijngū, Tateyama, Chiba 294-0233 | website = {{Official|1=http://www.awajinjya.org|qq=}} | architecture_style = | festival = August 10 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Awa Shrine'''|安房神社|Awa Jinja|qq=}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the Daijingū neighborhood of the city of [[Tateyama, Chiba|Tateyama]] in [[Chiba Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of two shrines claiming to hold the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Awa Province (Chiba)|Awa Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on August 10.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese|qq=}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Awa Jinja is: *{{nihongo|'''[[Futodama|Ame-no-Futodama-no-mikoto]]'''|天太玉命|qq=}}, the ancestor of Imbe clan The secondary ''kami'' enshrined at Awa Jinja are: * {{nihongo|'''Ame-no-hiritome-no-mikoto'''|天乃比理刀咩命|qq=}}, consort of Ame-no-Futodama *{{nihongo|'''[[Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Kushiakarutama-no-mikoto]]'''|櫛明玉命|qq=}}, ancestor of the Izumo Imbe clan, the creator of [[Yasakani no Magatama]] *{{nihongo|'''Ame-no-hiwashi-no-mikoto'''|天日鷲命|qq=}}, ancestor of the Awa Imbe clan *{{nihongo|'''Taokihooi-no-mikoto'''|手置帆負命|qq=}}, ancestor of the Saga Imbe clan *{{nihongo|'''Hikosashiri-no-mikoto'''|彦狭知命|qq=}},, ancestor of the Kii Imbe clan *{{nihongo|'''[[Ame-no-mahitotsu-no-mikoto]]'''|[[天目一箇命]]|qq=}}, ancestor of the [[Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi]] and Ise Imbe clans ==History== The date of Awa Shrine’s foundation is unknown. Shrine tradition and the ''[[Kogo Shūi]]'' records of 807 AD gives the founder as a member of the [[Inbe clan]], (the precursors to the [[Nakatomi clan]]) during the reign of the legendary [[Emperor Jimmu]], who settled in this area from [[Awa Province (Tokushima)|Awa Province]] in [[Shikoku]]. The shrine is mentioned several times in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Rikkokushi]]'' and it is mentioned again in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. It was patronized by [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]] in the [[Kamakura period]] and was recognized as the ''ichinomiya'' of the province since around this time. The shrine was completely destroyed by a large earthquake in 1499, and was rebuilt in 1593 by [[Satomi Yoshinari]]. The [[Satomi clan]] rebuilt the shrine again in 1536 and around 1592. Subsequently, in the [[Edo Period]] it was granted stipends from the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] in 1616 and 1637. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the Awa Shrine was designated the Rank of {{nihongo|Imperial shrine, 1st Rank |国幣大社|Kokuhei Taisha|qq=}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] under [[State Shinto]] in 1875.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. The present ''[[Honden]]'' dates from 1881 and was renovated in 2009.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese|qq=}} During archaeological investigations in 1931, numerous [[Jōmon period|Jōmon]] and [[Kofun period]] artifacts were recovered, including a number of [[bronze mirror]]s, clay vessels and the skeletons of 22 people.{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-037.html|title=安房神社洞窟遺跡|trans-title=Abo Shrine Cave Ruins|language=Japanese |publisher=Chiba Prefecture |accessdate=August 20, 2020|qq=}} The shrine is a ten-kilometer walk from [[Tateyama Station (Chiba)|Tateyama Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Uchibo Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese|qq=}} ==Gallery== Awa-jinja, shaden.JPG|Shaden Awa-jinja, honden.JPG|Honden Awa-jinja, ichi-no-torii.JPG|Ichi-no-torii Awa-jinja, keidai.JPG|Precincts Awa-ijnja, shinsenjo.JPG|Shinen-jo Awa-jinja, Inbe-zuka.JPG|Imbe-zuka Awa-jinja, ni-no-torii.JPG|Ni-no-Torii ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==Notes== {{Reflist|30em|qq=}} ==External links== *{{commons Category-inline|Awa-jinja|qq=}} *{{Official|1=http://www.awajinjya.org|qq=}} ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. (1996). ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. London: [[RoutledgeCurzon]]. {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8|qq=}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). ''[https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ The Imperial House of Japan]''.'' Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|194887|qq=}} {{Shinto shrine|qq=}}{{Inbe Faith|qq=}} {{Beppyo Shrines|qq=}} {{Ichinomiya|qq=}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Awa Shrine]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] '''Awa Shrine (Q705547)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Chiba)]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Awa District, Chiba|en|Awa District, Chiba|ja|安房郡|ceb|Awa-gun (distrito sa Hapon)|fa|شهرستان آوا، چیبا|fr|District d'Awa|id|Distrik Awa, Chiba|it|Distretto di Awa|ko|아와군|pl|Powiat Awa|ru|Ава (уезд)|th|อำเภออาวะ (จังหวัดชิบะ)|uk|Повіт Ава (Тіба)|zh|安房郡|lt=Awa district|qid=Q1153597|1=Awa District, Chiba}} || {{ill|Awa Shrine|lt=Awa Shrine|en|Awa Shrine|ja|安房神社|commons|Category:Awa-jinja|fr|Awa-jinja|zh|安房神社|qid=Q705547|1=Awa Shrine}} || {{ill|Kanpei-sha|qid=Q135160338|lt=Kanpei|comment=categorized|1=Kanpei-sha}} ({{ill|Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings|qid=Q135009132|lt=Tsukinami|1=Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings}}{{efn|gets offerings for the yearly {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|ja|新嘗祭|qid=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} and the monthly {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|simple|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|qid=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}}.}}) || {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} || {{ill|ichinomiya|qid=Q1656379|lt=ichinomiya|1=Ichinomiya}} || — || {{coord|34.922224|139.836581|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181732 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q705547}} [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Chiba)]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior Third Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Third Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Awa Province (Chiba)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Chiba)]] [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] [[Category:Inbe clan]] [[Category:Top 500 Attractions of Bōsō Peninsula]] [[Category:Shinmei-zukuri]] [[Category:Zen Clan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tateyama, Chiba]] [[Category:History of Tateyama, Chiba]]
